wonder: Can I follow or adapt instructions to build a physical object?
activity_summary: Learner used physical pieces and visual instructions to construct a build. The activity involved sequencing, part-to-whole reasoning, spatial awareness, and manual execution.
learner_explanation: I found the pieces and put them together to make the build.

parent_review.review_note: Accepted as evidence of spatial reasoning, instruction-following, construction, persistence, and translation from image to object.

subject_mappings.subject: engineering
subject_mappings.evidence_note: Learner assembled parts into a functional or representational structure.

subject_mappings.subject: mathematics
subject_mappings.evidence_note: Learner used spatial relationships, sequence, comparison, and part matching.

subject_mappings.subject: art
subject_mappings.evidence_note: Learner created a visual and physical object.

evidence_items.description: Completed or partially completed physical build.
Photo of the build may be attached.

next_questions: What part was hardest to find or place?
What would you change if you built your own version?
Can the build be made stronger, taller, faster, or more detailed?
non_capture_note: This receipt records one learning event and should not be used as a fixed identity label.
